data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-SpaceX’s nascent monopoly faces durability test
data.snippet: Achieving a valuation of $1 trln or more is only a first step for the rockets‑and‑satellites group. Post IPO, rivals like Amazon will roll out competing broadband services, threatening its market share and pricing power. SpaceX may then face a tighter supply of financial fuel.

image.text: SpaceX logo is seen in this illustration taken, March 10, 2025.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Janus Henderson interloper ramps up fund austerity
data.snippet: Victory Capital gatecrashed the storied asset manager’s agreed sale to pushy investor Trian with a $9 bln cash-and-stock offer. Take an eyebrow-raising $500 mln of cost-cuts at face value, and it’s clearly superior. It’s just a lot to more to chop out of a consolidating business.

image.text: The Wall Street sign is pictured at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in the Manhattan borough of New York City, New York, U.S., March 9, 2020.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Nvidia is dogged by memories of cycles past
data.snippet: The $5 trln AI chipmaker’s revenue keeps growing at a phenomenal rate, 73% in its last quarter. Yet for six months, its stock has stalled while supplier SK Hynix's has nearly quadrupled. When investors worry that a maturing boom will eat early profits, valuations tighten.

image.text: An Nvidia logo appears in this illustration created on August 25, 2025.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Rolls-Royce valuation can remain in the heavens
data.snippet: The $150 bln engine maker’s stock rose 6% after bumper results, bringing the gains since Tufan Erginbilgic became CEO in 2023 to over 1,300%. Aircraft shortages, booming travel, data centres and buybacks can sustain the surge – a far cry from the ‘burning platform’ he inherited.

image.text: A BR700-725 jet engine is seen at the assembly line of the Rolls-Royce Germany plant in Dahlewitz near Berlin, Germany, February 28, 2023.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-LSEG's Elliott-lite playbook may just be enough
data.snippet: The $57 bln data-to-exchange group announced a share buyback alongside new growth and margin targets. It should help to assuage pushy investor Elliott. Big moves like a breakup look unlikely, as long as CEO David Schwimmer can keep reviving the depressed stock.

image.text: Signage is seen outside the LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) headquarters in Paternoster Square, London, Britain, April 25, 2025.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-‘The Big Short’ confab makes merry amid credit storm
data.snippet: Wall Street may be buried in snow, but the mood this week at SFVegas — the annual conclave of financiers made infamous by Michael Lewis’s "The Big Short" — was strikingly sunny. Accumulating worries about credit cockroaches or software-sector blowups barely clouded the outlook. Instead, 10,000 attendees crowded the halls of Sin City’s structured-finance gathering to celebrate Wall Street’s continued mania for byzantine IOUs, treating recent upsets as opportunities for ever more slicing and dicing.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Li clan amps up quest for a simple sprawl discount
data.snippet: Hong Kong billionaire Li Ka-shing's empire is selling UK Power to French utility Engie for $14 bln. It frees up capital for more deals. With CK flagships trading at just 40% of book value, however, finding a better investment than returning it to shareholders will be tough.

image.text: Hong Kong tycoon Li Ka-shing listens to his son Victor Li during a news conference in Hong Kong, China, March 16, 2018.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Salesforce's problem is not enough AI upheaval
data.snippet: AI displacement fears have hit shares of the $177 bln software firm. It sees a return to accelerating organic growth by embracing computer ‘agents’ that do the work of people. But they haven’t replaced humans yet, so big gains aren't showing. Disruption is too fast and too slow.

image.text: The Salesforce Tower New York building is pictured in Manhattan in New York City, U.S., April 14, 2023.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Kevin Warsh can work with the Fed to shrink it
data.snippet: Trump’s pick to lead the central bank argues for trimming its most powerful tools. First task: win over institutionalists leery of White House bullying, who could block his agenda. From there, he could well remold a post-crisis Federal Reserve and its $6.6 trln balance sheet.

image.text: Kevin Warsh speaks during a monetary policy conference at Stanford University?s Hoover Institution in Palo Alto, California, U.S. May 9, 2025.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Ellisons tempt Netflix into a game of M&A chicken
data.snippet: David, son of Oracle founder Larry Ellison, upped his $108 bln offer for Warner Bros, effectively promising his father’s stock fortune will backstop the bid. Putting the clan's tech empire on the line pressures rival Netflix to de-risk its complex proposal.

image.text: A Paramount sign is seen outside their offices at 1515 Broadway in New York City, U.S., February 20, 2026.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-PayPal pursuit confronts a seller's dilemma
data.snippet: The $44 bln payments pioneer has attracted interest from potential suitors including Stripe, Bloomberg reported. The unlisted upstart's richer valuation means it could pay up and still profit. But PayPal's scale, strong cash flow and beaten-down stock make any deal a tough sell.

image.text: The PayPal logo is seen on a smartphone in front of the same logo displayed in this illustration taken September 8, 2021.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Software rout exacerbates buyout exit crunch
data.snippet: Hg-backed Visma is delaying its IPO until at least 2027, as the AI-driven selloff depresses tech valuations. It follows similar moves by Blackstone. Private equity groups that spent over $220 bln on a now-embattled sector will find it harder to return cash to investors.

image.text: Figurines with computers and smartphones are seen in front of the words "Artificial Intelligence AI" in this illustration created on February 19, 2024.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Santander's top-tier status looks hard to dislodge
data.snippet: The $191 bln Spanish bank led by Ana Botín now trades at a comfortable premium to its book value, in the highest valued group of European peers. That’s down to savvy M&A, rigorous efficiency, and a cleaner business mix. A plausible 20% return target leaves scope for upside.

image.text: Ana Botin, head of Santander, speaks during the ReutersNEXT Newsmaker event in New York City, New York, U.S., November 9, 2023.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-HSBC valuation uplift relies on tough vibe shift
data.snippet: The lender has surpassed $300 bln in market value, but lags the $400 bln level an executive recently suggested was possible. New, higher return goals won’t suffice, meaning it will need a JPMorgan-style multiple too. That looks tricky given higher political and economic risks.

image.text: HSBC Bank logo is seen in this illustration taken March 12, 2023.

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Trump’s tariff upset has plenty of upside
data.snippet: The US may be on the hook for $175 bln worth of refunds after the Supreme Court deemed the president’s tariffs illegal. In this Viewsroom podcast, Breakingviews columnists discuss the opportunities for countries now eyeing a weakened Trump and what’s likely to come next.

image.text: A 3D-printed miniature model depicting U.S. President Donald Trump, U.S. flag and word "Tariffs" in this illustration taken,  April 17, 2025.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
